1.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the
following adjusting journal entries:

Trust Fund- KALAHI-CIDSS NCDDP
1. To record the fund transfer of LGU’s counterpart on July 31,
2021.
Prior Period Adjustment ₱2,246,673.00
Trust Liabilities ₱2,246,673.00
2. To adjust the erroneously recorded transactions as expense
instead of deduction to Trust Liabilities
Trust Liabilities ₱1,754,039.00
Prior Period Adjustment ₱1,754,039.00

Trust Fund Proper
1. To adjust the erroneously recorded transactions as expense
instead of deduction to Trust Liabilities
Trust Liabilities ₱80,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment ₱80,000.00

Likewise, she agreed to use the appropriate account in recording the
succeeding transactions in the Trust Fund books.

2.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination
with the Barangay Bookkeeper, trace the recipient Barangays’
financial assistance-related disbursement vouchers, reconcile these
documents with the existing accounting records, and make the
necessary adjusting journal entries in the accounting books to
reflect the accurate status of the Due from LGUs account in the
Statement of Financial Position. Furthermore, require the concerned
Barangays to submit complete liquidation reports as separate
documents from their monthly disbursement reports and accounts,
and to diligently prepare or update the Reports of Appropriations
and Obligations (RAO) for the timely monitoring of Barangay funds,
including those received for specific purposes.

3.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary
adjusting entries to recognize the income collected in prior year as a
prior period adjustment in the Equity account in order not to
understate the affected income accounts in CY 2024; and the
Municipal Treasurer report all cash collections in the period to which
they are collected as provided in Sections 29 and 35 of NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume 1 and forward the same to the Accounting
Office to properly account in the Agency’s books.

4.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw an
adjusting entry to recognize in the books the appropriate PPE
account for the foregoing repair of the rescue vehicle amounting to
₱196,500.00. Likewise, capitalize expenditures in accordance with
Paragraphs 13, 14, 23 and 24 of IPSAS No. 1.

5.9 We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer and the
Municipal Accountant agreed to fill in the account code section of
the Certification on Appropriations, Funds, and Obligations of
Allotment (CAFOA), and to make necessary adjustments and record
transactions under its appropriate accounts, respectively, to ensure
the fair presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements
pursuant to IPSASs 1 and proper classification of transactions in
accordance with COA Circular 2015-009.

6.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting journal entries in the accounting books to
reclassify the total cost incurred to date of the ongoing development
project, Construction of Multi-Purpose Building at Barangay Mabini,
amounting to ₱4,914,721.40, in the “Construction in Progress-
Infrastructure Asset” account and record the same amount in the
“Construction in Progress-Buildings and Other Structures” account
to correct the misstatements and ensure the fair presentation of
these CIP accounts in the Statement of Financial Position. Likewise,
ensure to use appropriate accounts for ongoing projects in the
succeeding transactions.

7.15 We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to focus on
completing the physical inventory of Property, Plant, and Equipment
(PPE), to reconcile any discrepancies between the book balances
and the results of the physical count with the Municipal Accountant,
and to ensure the full implementation of the "One-Time Cleansing of
PPE accounts" as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Additionally, the Committee promptly furnish the Auditor with copies
of the inventory report and the reconciliation results for further
review and assessment.

observations.recommendation:
1.11	We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a.	The Municipal Accountant draw JEV to derecognize the PPE amounting to ₱3,182,079.64 in the Trust Fund Books and record the same in the General Fund Books of Accounts;
b.	The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GS Officer reconcile the RPCPPE with that appearing in the Financial Statements in order fully establish the accuracy, completeness and existence of the PPE account in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standards; and
c.	Facilitate the implementation of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

2.9	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to effect the necessary adjusting journal entries in the Books of accounts to correct the misstatements. Prepare and maintain the required Trust Liability-DRRMF Subsidiary Ledgers, and adhere to the guidelines cited above in the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF for the succeeding years.

3.8	We recommended the Municipal Accountant prepare a Disbursing Voucher to return the remaining balance of the fund to the National Treasury in compliance with Item 3.9 of Local Budget Circular 125 dated April 7, 2020.

4.11	We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a.	the MDRRM Officer furnish the MPD Council with the approved revised/supplemental LDRRMFIP which includes the new PPAs to be charged against the unexpended prior year balances of the LDRRMF;
b.	with that as the basis, the MPD Council update/revise the AIP of the Municipality to adopt the changes in the LDRRMFIP; and
c.	Adhere strictly to Item 4.4 and 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002 and Section 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recordings and classification of the accounts booked in the Due to NGAs and update the records in the subsidiary ledger to bring the account into a corrected balance.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I on setting up RPT/SET Receivable; the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant. With that as the basis, the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to  take up in the books the RPT/SET Receivable and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

We recommended that Management strictly observed the provisions under Section 22 and Section 77 of Republic Act 7160 as regards to the hiring of COS/JOs in order to validate the legality and necessity of COS/JOs hired and to be hired in the Municipality and to support the propriety in the payment of their wages out of government fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to revisit and tailor fit the provisions and stipulations of the contract in accordance with the respective function and responsibilities of the COS/JO in particular, to ensure the necessity and purpose of engaging their services; and have the perfected contract notarized.

We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer the timely preparation and submission of reports as required under COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 to ensure transparency and proper accounting of LDRRM Funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	require the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineer’s Office, and all other concerned offices compliance with the provisions of The Updated 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and the GPPB Circular 04-2020 in the conduct of public bidding and awarding of the contracts in the procurement of infrastructure projects; and

b.	require the winning bidders/contractors to submit the lacking documents on the perfected contracts; and submit a reconciled and/ or amended documents to reconcile the discrepancies noted, for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended the Management:

a.	To stop the manual payment of allowances, monthly RATA, payroll, midyear and year-end bonus and all other benefits and incentives of the employees, instead, fully adopt the existing Automated Payroll System to facilitate the direct crediting to employees’ bank accounts to prevent delay and avoid the risk of loss or misappropriation of public funds that are inherent in a manual system of disbursement.


b.	To also adopt and apply the existing Automated Payroll System for Job Order Personnel.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the use of ATM for all payments of benefits to all regular employees. However, all payments to Job Order employees were still made through cash basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Supply Officer/Property Officer Designate ensure the timely conduct of physical count on the inventories of the Municipality and prepare a report on the result of physical count.

We recommended that the Management purchase sound system that is suitable to government related seminars, activities and celebrations instead of renting the same to save renting expenses in the future.
observations.reason: The Management still renting sound system, however on a minimal amount only since there was no big event that had been implemented due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Office to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Municipal Accounting Office’s records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs; ensure the conduct of physical count of the LGU’s inventories; and the preparation and maintenance of Stock Cards and Property Cards. Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and maintain Supply Ledger Cards and PPE Ledgers Cards and ensure that the reconciliation of records is done before the preparation of the annual financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the contents of outstanding checks and coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to furnish their office with the list of checks staled and cancelled or checks staled but reissued together with the original disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents to facilitate accurate preparation of journal entry vouchers and ensure proper recording of the same.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary liquidation report to DOH immediately for the funds received and utilized;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to return the excess or unutilized fund in the amount of ₱ 1,054.16 to the DOH - HFEP; and
Advice the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the accounts Due to NGAs and Government Equity accounts to ensure fair presentation in the Financial Statements.

We recommended to make representation with the management of Cagdianao Mining Corporation, present other options and strategies in utilizing the funds and renegotiate for amendments or modifications of the terms and conditions of the MOA to avoid reversal of the donated fund.

We recommended that the local treasurer request an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to close the Municipality’s depository account at LBP-Surigao City Branch and to open the depository accounts to LBP-San Jose, Dinagat Branch in view of ensuring the efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of the Municipality, and to avoid incurrence of additional cost and risk while transacting with depository bank.

We recommended to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit to the office of the Auditor all perfected contracts within five (5) working days upon approval, as mandated under COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009, so that timely evaluation can be made by the office.

We recommended that the MDRRM Officer furnish the MPDC the approved supplemental and/or revised LDRRMFIP which includes the PPAs to be charged against the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF. This would be the basis of the MPDC to update/revise the AIP of the Municipality to adopt the changes in the LDRRMFIP. Adhere strictly to Section 2.5.6 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 24 and Section 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the MSWD Office prepare and submit to COA Office the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2021 and ensure the preparation and submission of the said report for the following years in accordance with the PCW, NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01. Likewise, we recommended the Management to designate a MSWD Officer to effectively manage and supervise the implementation of the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records on the complete list of all on-going projects submitted by the Municipal Engineer with that of 	the subsidiary ledger of the Municipal Accountant, and any records deficiencies must be reconciled to bring this account into agreement and to ascertain the validity and propriety of the recorded Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts in the Financial Statement.

We recommended that the Property Officer, prepare Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, supported with Acknowledgement Receipt, Issuance Form and inventory of remaining undistributed items for prospective COVID-19 related in-kind donations received within ten (10) working days after the end of each quarter in compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-009. Refrain from delegating the end-user the responsibility to receive and dispose the subject donations in order to achieve sound internal control and proper accounting thereof.

We recommended that the BAC check the eligibility requirements of the prospective bidders and post the required procurement information in the website of the Municipality and/or in at least three conspicuous places in the premises of the procuring entity and at GPPB online portal in adherence to the provisions of the above - mentioned Circular.

Also, the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before the submission of DVs for approval for payment by the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee diligently prepare the monthly utilization report for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities and post the same to the LGU’s website and in at least three conspicuous places in the locality. Also send written notices, that said reports were posted, to the respective government agencies where the submission is required in compliance with Items 4.2 to 4.4 of DBM LBC 125 in order to properly monitor and establish transparency in the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant demand from the claimants the above – enumerated documents and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for re – evaluation to prove the propriety, validity and reasonableness of the claims.

We recommended that the CMPS personnel provide justification and other documents that will prove the legality of receiving hazard pay from the Municipality and submit the same to the office of the auditor for re – evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant release to the barangays the collected shares in RPT within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271 of Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommended the use of the new format of the Disbursement Voucher as provided in the Government Accounting Manual, Volume II, embodying therein the journal entries to record the financial transactions. 
 
For the DVs in the previous transactions, submit immediately the JEVs to the office of the Auditor for verification on the authenticity of the entries made to record the financial transactions.

We recommended that Management attach the prescribed documents in the disbursement of fuel as provided in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.

Also submit the monthly reports of fuel consumption supported with the duly approved driver’s trip tickets and monthly reports of official travel for re - evaluation of the transactions.
We recommended to ensure that solid waste materials are deposited in appropriate and environmentally sound facilities in accordance with Section 2 of RA 9003.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO shall ensure the timely preparation and submission of the required reports in accordance with COA Circular Nos.    2012-002 and 2014-002.

Moreover, the use of government vehicles shall be properly authorized through a driver’s trip ticket, which should be serially numbered and should bear the approval of the Head of Office, or his authorized representative in case of his absence. observations.recommendation: 1.7	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the affected accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in order to present fairly the Due to Other Funds and Due From Other Funds accounts in the year-end FS.

2.10	We recommended that the Municipal Assessor’s Office, in coordination with the Provincial Assessors Office, update the assessed values of the 441 unassessed real properties taking into consideration the issues encountered during the latest revision and ensure that the same will be addressed.

3.7	We recommended that Management compel the concerned BLGUs   to effect the immediate liquidation of the funds transferred thereto by not making subsequent fund transfers to their favor unless previous fund transfers were fully liquidated.

4.5	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant work on the immediate remittance of the amount stated above to the BIR in order to avert the imposition of additional penalties and surcharges due to non-payment of tax withheld.

5.7 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure timely remittance of the amount due to the afore-cited government corporations to avert the imposition of penalty and surcharges and in order not to prejudice the Government as well as the personnel concerned.

6.9	We recommended that Management instruct the concerned personnel to substantiate fuel utilization through proper accomplishment of the Drivers Trip Tickets, disclosing all relevant information therein, in order that appropriate monitoring and control can be effected and to avert wastage of government resourced.
7.5	We recommended that Management appropriate sufficient funds to effect the procurement of a sound system in order to avoid the incurrence of excessive expenditures.
8.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the Php5,000.00 net take home pay for each employee to comply with the afore-mentioned guidelines.

9.6	We recommended that Management establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System, as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA, in order to provide management with the much-needed data & information essential for policy making.
We recommended that the municipal accountant reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds Accounts to present fairly the financial position of the municipality.

Direct the Management to revise the Solid Waste Management Plan by including in it the plan of collecting the non-recyclable materials and special wastes of the three (3) barangays previously not included and assure implementation of the said plan in conformity with Section 10 of RA 9003.

Direct the Management to prioritize inclusion in the Project Procurement Management Plan of the ensuing year the personal protective equipment and supplies of the garbage collectors in compliance with Section 23(a) of RA 9003.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Systems appropriately identify PAPs that are gender-responsive and gender-sensitive for inclusion to its GAD Plan and stop disbursing GAD Funds to PAPs that are not related to gender and development.

We also recommended that payment for philhealth premiums of the indigents be charged to the appropriate fund of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports/documents enumerated in Section 4.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.

Direct Management to stop the practice of appropriating and implementing projects under the 20% DF not found in the LDP.

Likewise, proper planning and coordination among concerned officials and employees be put in place to avoid recurrence of the aforementioned violation.

Require Management to stop the practice of appropriating and implementing PAPs charged to the national wealth that did not contain the purpose of local development and livelihood. 

Likewise, reprogram the existing PAPs identified to PAPs that are qualified to the purposes of the usage of the national wealth.
Direct the MPDO to submit the AIP regularly to the DILG Regional Office and DBM in compliance with Section 6 of the afore-cited JMC.
Direct management to include in the LDRRMP of the ensuing year activities and projects under disaster rehabilitation and post recovery which are not yet included in the previous plan and budget.

We recommended that management require the claimants of honoraria as lecturers, resource persons, coordinators and facilitators of the necessary documentary requirements and strictly adhere to the guidelines provided on the grant of the said honoraria.

We recommended that Management direct the municipal accountant to require the concerned offices charging repairs and maintenance to completely attach the documentary requirements required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before approving the same.

We recommend that Management direct the Authorized Agency Officer and Electronic Remittance File Officers to act prudently in approving the loans of GSIS member-employees and remit the required monthly amortizations to the GSIS promptly and correctly in accordance with the amortization schedule.

Direct Management to advise the BAC to avoid direct contracting of goods and services which should be done thru negotiated procurement as provided under the provisions of the Procurement Law.

Likewise, as much as possible and practicable, refrain from allowing the cooperative to participate in the procurement process if the cooperative is not the eligible supplier of the goods and services to be procured.

We recommended that Management appropriately identify PAPs that are gender-responsive and gender-sensitive for inclusion to its GAD Plan and stop disbursing GAD Funds to PAPs that are not related to gender and development.

Direct Management to completely adhere to the guidelines set by DSWD in the implementation of the AICS program. If possible, a resolution or executive order can be issued by the LGU to set a clear-cut, systematic and uniform rules and procedures of the AICS which must be aligned with the above-cited guidelines.
Direct the concerned officials and employees to exert enough effort to find another source of water and/or install a water treatment facility, such that, potability of water could be assured.

observations.recommendation: We recommend giving equal priority to the implementation of the construction, repair and maintenance of school buildings and facilities, educational research, sports development, and purchased of books and periodicals to benefit the targeted recipients.

observations.recommendation:
Advise the accountable officials and employees concerned to immediately liquidate their unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, withhold their salaryies and apply other sanctions pursuant to paragraph 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant not to grant advances to elected officials other than for travelling expenses and strictly comply with Sections 4.1.2 to 4.1.4 of the aforementioned circular.

If accountable officers are no longer connected with the LGU, send demand letters and/or require them to settle first their accountabilities before issuing a clearance, if not yet cleared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the reconciliation of PPE balances on both records of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer. Proper indication on the serviceability of the properties shall likewise be made to identify those items that require to be depreciated, and be classified to accordingly. Unserviceable properties shall be appraised and be subsequently disposed following the procedures and modes of disposal prescribed in the Manual on Disposal issued by this Commission.

Regular conduct of reconciliation of property records by both municipal officials shall be strictly implemented and the necessary adjustments, if any, shall be effected in the books of accounts. Compliance to the accounting rules and regulations shall be strictly observed to avoid any misstatement in the financial statements.

Moreover, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PC and PPELC, respectively, for all items under PPE. Periodic reconciliation of records shall be undertaken to avoid any misstatement in the quantity and amount of properties.

Require refund of the amount paid to Atty. Bernard L. Dagcuta for unauthorized legal services at the LGU.  Henceforth, a written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and a written concurrence of the Commission on Audit must first be secured before engaging the services of a private lawyer by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The concerned was already informed and promised to refund the said amount.
observations.reason: No refund was made during the year.

2.7 Likewise, reconciliation between the records of the municipal accountant and the municipal assessor should be made from time to time to accurately establish the valuation of land in the book of accounts of the Municipality.

10.5  Direct Management to:
10.5.1 Formulate policy/limitation on the granting of financial assistance as to how much will be given to the constituents. Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents.
10.5.2  Moreover, coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to assess request for medical and hospitalization assistance way of Social Case Study Reports so that only the deserving indigents can be assisted and served.
10.5.3  Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to require the applicants for AICS a complete submission of supporting documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment and recommendation to make sure that only the deserving ones were provided with the necessary assistance to help them cope during crisis situations.
10.5.4  Lastly, submit the lacking documentary requirements itemized in Annex H to avoid suspensions in audit.
11.5 Instruct the MDRRMO and the MSWDO to stockpile food packs   which are adequate enough to cater the constituents’ needs during and after disasters and calamities.

We recommended that Management execute a MOA with
the Provincial Government of Dinagat Islands and with the recipient-barangays. A copy of the MOAs shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. Every financial assistance granted thereafter shall be supported with a MOA. For financial assistance granted to barangays, these shall be recorded in the books of accounts as receivable to compel the recipient-barangays to liquidate the funds transferred within the prescribed period to ensure that the funds are utilized for the intended purpose(s) and the implementation of which are actually undertaken.
observations.managementAction: Financial assistance was now recorded as receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in CY 2016 by taking up in the books of accounts the grant of financial assistance using the following entry:


Due from NGOs/POs	      XXX

   Prior Years’ Adjustments XXX

We also recommended that Management require the concerned officers and employees of the recipient NGOs/POs to promptly submit the audited report on the liquidation of the fund released to them, and require the Municipal Accountant to record upon submission the audited liquidation report in the books.

Furthermore, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to properly record the transactions using the appropriate accounts, recognize expenses only when incurred, and comply with pertinent accounting and audit rules and regulations on the grant of financial assistance to NGOs/POs.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and regularly compute for depreciation on all depreciable assets. It shall be computed using the straight-line method and in accordance with the other provisions on depreciation under PPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: Some PPEs were depreciated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging expenditures that are not related to projects on social and economic development, and environmental outcomes, and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures to the 20% development fund. Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

Furthermore, expenditures such as 
labor, direct materials and overhead pertaining to the construction, rehabilitation or repair of agency assets/government structures, which could be an addition, could contribute to the betterment, or could extend the life of such assets, should be capitalized.
observations.managementAction: Management did not charge to 20% expenses not related to the socio-economic and environmental outcomes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all subsequent payments of wages to job order workers as MOOE and NOT as Salaries and Wages-Casual/Contractual under PS. Compliance with the existing accounting rules on the proper use of accounts shall be observed to enhance the presentation of the accounts and avoid any misstatement in the financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Wages of job orders were now charged to MOOE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure that fuel purchased by the Municipality should only be issued to government vehicles. In no case shall it be fueled to privately owned motor vehicles. Direct the refund of the fuel expenses paid, which was issued to privately-owned vehicles. 

Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to examine all driver’s trip tickets to ensure that the fuel purchased are loaded only to government vehicles. Moreover, the use of government vehicles shall be properly authorized through a driver’s trip ticket, which should be serially numbered and should bear the approval of the Head of Office, or his authorized representative in case of his absence. Only originally signed trip tickets shall be submitted to the COA. 

Furthermore, ensure that all payments of fuel expenses are completely documented with these trip tickets, and instruct the drivers to properly and completely fill-up all the necessary data therein such as the no. of liters of fuel purchased, utilized and unused before and after the trip. Data on speedometer readings shall likewise be disclosed. These shall be summarized at the end of every month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels and a monthly report of fuel consumption, and be submitted to the Office of the Auditor to enable the audit of fuel expenses.
observations.managementAction: Refunded the disallowed amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to submit the required complete list to enable the Auditor to validate the existence and status of implementation of the related projects. The subsidiary ledgers of the subject accounts shall also be updated, a copy of which shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for reevaluation and validation. Likewise, promptly submit on a quarterly basis the list of publicized programs, activities and projects as required in the above-cited COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: Submitted List of publicised PAPs quarterly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management send demand letters to concerned employees who were granted with cash advances for travel, as listed in the schedule appended herewith as Annex “B”, requiring them to liquidate their outstanding cash advances. Ensure proper monitoring of cash advances granted to officials and employees, and exhaust administrative efforts by issuing memoranda to officials and employees requiring them to settle immediately all cash advances granted to them within the prescribed period to liquidate.

Likewise, a certification from the Municipal Accountant shall be issued for subsequent grant of special cash advances, which shall be attached to the disbursement vouchers relative to such grant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts to bring the balances of the said inventory accounts to correct balances. Regular conduct of the semestral physical count shall be made thereafter, and periodic reconciliation of accounting records and records maintained by the Municipal Treasurer or the concerned personnel designated as supply officer shall be undertaken.

We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain SC and SLC, respectively, for all items under inventory. Periodic reconciliation of records shall be undertaken to avoid any misstatement in the quantity and amount of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has effected some of the necessary adjustments pertaining to inventories in the books of accounts. SCs and SLCs were not yet updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical inventory-taking of all items included under PPE, as well as those items considered as small tangible items, regularly to establish the existence and proper valuation of recorded assets, and fairly report the balances of the PPE accounts. Selective inventory-taking of property shall not allowed.

Furthermore, periodic reconciliation of accounting records and records maintained by the property records be undertaken. Disposal of all unserviceable properties and those beyond economic repair should also be made to achieve fair presentation of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Likewise, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PC and PPELC, respectively, for all items under PPE. Periodic reconciliation of records shall be undertaken to avoid any misstatements in the quantity and amount of inventories and properties.
observations.managementAction: Management has directed the conduct of annual physical count of PPE and a RPCPPE was submitted to the COA. Discrepancies still existed between the records of both Municipal Accountant and Property Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and regularly compute for depreciation on all depreciable assets. It shall be computed using the straight-line method, which shall start on the following month after the purchase/completion of the PPE, irrespective of the date within the month.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation on several PPE items were computed by the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging expenditures that are not related to projects on social and economic development, and environmental outcomes, and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures to the 20% development fund. Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Construction Period Theory be followed in its application for costing purposes on construction of agency assets and public infrastructures. During the construction period, these shall be taken up in the books as "Construction in Progress” with the appropriate asset classification.  As soon as the project is completed, the Construction in Progress for agency asset shall be closed to the appropriate asset account.

Likewise, expenditures such as freight and handling of heavy equipment in bringing the asset to its intended use, or any payment of labor, direct materials and overhead pertaining to the construction, rehabilitation or repair of agency assets/government structures, which could be an addition, could contribute to the betterment, or could extend the life of such assets, should be capitalized.
observations.managementAction: Management did not charge to 20% expenses not related to the socio-economic and environmental outcomes.

Notices to payees who have custody of checks which will become stale, shall be sent by the Municipal Treasurer at least one (1) month before same become stale to inform them of the status of the checks.
